input.prompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.content: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

<current_datetime>2026-05-13T11:27:51.795Z</current_datetime>

I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand what we're working with.

6.   <ItemGroup>
7.     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />

9.     <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />

11. </Project>
12.

data.content: This is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. I'll start `dotnet watch test` which re-runs your tests automatically whenever you save a file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET 8 MSTest project with failing tests. They want to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload to iterate faster. Let me set that up.

The express test expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m, so that's the failing test. But the user asked me to set up hot reload, not fix the tests.

`dotnet watch test` is the command that will re-run tests automatically when files change. Let me start that up for them.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-89639217f5864a3194d108bb96cda1f8 && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "dotnet-watch"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.content: `dotnet watch` is running and watching for changes. There's a build error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix that so the tests can compile and run.
data.reasoningText: There's a compilation error - `ArgumentException` needs `System` namespace. The file is missing `using System;`. Let me fix that and the watch will automatically re-run.

data.content:
Hot reload is up and running! `dotnet watch test` is now watching for file changes and will automatically re-run your tests on every save.

Here's the current test status:
- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The fix needed is in `CalculateShipping`: the `"express"` case returns `10.00m` but the test expects `15.00m`. Edit the file and the watcher will re-run automatically. I also added the missing `using System;` to fix a build error.
